sections.paragraph:
εὔμορφος, ον (Sappho 82a [comp.]; Trag., Hdt. et al.; POxy 471, 79; 109) well-formed, beautiful of women (Artem. 1, 78 p. 73, 9 al.; Xenophon Eph. 1, 4, 3; Sir 9:8; Philo, Virt. 110; TestJud 13:3; TestSol; TestAbr A; Just., D. 134, 1.—εὐμορφία Jos., Ant. 15, 23) Hs 9, 13, 8.—DELG s.v. μορφή.

Sappho Sappho , VII–VI b.c.—List 5

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 409
